entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum, the crop species used to develop resistant and early maturing germplasms

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA BLIGHT
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting chickpeas caused by Ascochyta rabiei

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA RABIEI
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing Ascochyta blight in chickpeas

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea germplasms to withstand infection by Ascochyta rabiei

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: A trait characterized by a shorter growth cycle, specifically ≤131 days to 50% flowering

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: A trait referring to the weight and dimensions of the seeds, specifically large seeds measuring 40-50.6 g per 100 seeds

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the crop, specifically high-yielding lines

entities.name: KABULI TYPE
entities.description: A specific chickpea seed type characterized by ram-head-shaped, large, beige seeds

entities.name: RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method used to increase the frequency of desirable traits like resistance to A. rabiei

entities.name: SYRIA
entities.description: The country where the breeding project and selection process took place

entities.name: TEL HADYA
entities.description: The specific location in Syria where recurrent selection was conducted

entities.name: ICRISAT-ICARDA KABULI CHICKPEA PROJECT
entities.description: A collaborative project aimed at developing Kabuli chickpea germplasms

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: A controlled environment used to evaluate the resistance of chickpea lines to A. rabiei

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: An open-air environment used to evaluate the resistance of chickpea lines to A. rabiei
